title: Abort + channel recreation on SSE reconnect: prevents stale StreamEnded on new channel
description: On SSE reconnect, the code aborts the old JoinHandle and creates a new (sse_tx, sse_rx) pair before re-spawning. Without the abort, the old task could still be executing and send StreamEnded into the new sse_rx after the new connection is already alive, triggering a spurious second reconnect cycle. Without the channel recreation, any messages queued in the old sse_rx (including that stale StreamEnded) would be visible to the new select\! arm. The two-step sequence — abort then drop+recreate channel — is the only correct solution. A future contributor might 'simplify' by just re-spawning on the same channel, or by skipping the abort assuming the old task will die on its own, introducing the double-reconnect bug documented in the spawn_sse_task comment.
